source: Manually curated snapshot · Data conceptually based on independent commodity assessors. Refreshed quarterly.
note: WTI grade differentials are the price gap between regional/grade-specific crude prices and Cushing benchmark. They're the cleanest signal of US oil flow imbalances.

differentials.name: WTI East Houston
differentials.context: When Houston trades above Cushing, US Gulf Coast refineries and exporters are paying up for crude. Persistent premiums indicate strong international export demand.

differentials.name: WTI Midland
differentials.context: Midland tracking Cushing closely indicates Permian Basin production is in rough balance with takeaway capacity. Sustained Midland discount signals Permian glut and pipeline constraints.

differentials.name: WTI Corpus Christi
differentials.context: Corpus Christi is the largest US crude export hub. A premium signals tankers loading for Asia/Europe and tight cargo availability at Gulf terminals.

differentials.name: Canadian Sour at Cushing
differentials.context: Western Canadian Select (WCS) trades at a structural discount due to higher density, sulfur content, and pipeline constraints out of Alberta. Wider discount = more heavy crude than refiners can absorb.
